purpose: Define discipline-facing applicability for governed admissibility testing.
core_question: What is this output, artifact, instruction, transition, or claim allowed to become?

disciplines.label: AI / LLM systems
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Model outputs can become public claims, tool calls, agent actions, automation inputs, or governance decisions.

disciplines.label: Mathematics / formal methods
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Proof attempts, derivations, solver artifacts, and counterexamples require posture before becoming claims.

disciplines.label: Software engineering
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Code suggestions and automation can become executed changes, deployments, or workflow actions.

disciplines.label: Cybersecurity / identity
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Credential use, access changes, identity assertions, and trust-boundary movement require explicit authority and consequence posture.

disciplines.label: Data governance
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Data movement changes privacy, evidence, provenance, and authority posture.

disciplines.label: Legal / policy
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Generated or summarized text may affect rights, obligations, compliance posture, or policy interpretation.

disciplines.label: Medicine / health
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Outputs may be interpreted as advice, risk classification, diagnosis support, or care instruction.

disciplines.label: Finance / markets
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Claims or generated actions can influence allocation, payment, risk, compliance, or investment behavior.

disciplines.label: Education / learning
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Outputs can shape assessment, curriculum, tutoring, learning posture, or claims about understanding.

disciplines.label: Science / research
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Hypotheses, summaries, analyses, and claims require source posture, reproducibility posture, and claim limits before publication.

disciplines.label: Engineering / infrastructure
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Recommendations may affect physical systems, operational continuity, maintenance, or safety posture.

disciplines.label: Robotics / autonomy
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Model or planner outputs may become physical-world actions or safety overrides.

disciplines.label: Journalism / public information
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Outputs may become public claims, source summaries, timelines, or narratives that shape public understanding.

disciplines.label: Government / civic systems
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Outputs may affect authority, eligibility, public services, civic trust, or administrative action.

disciplines.label: Organizational governance
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Approvals, overrides, exceptions, and board actions require authority, quorum, evidence, and consequence posture.

disciplines.label: Archives / records / history
disciplines.why_admissibility_applies: Timelines, source annotations, and historical claims require provenance, source posture, and review posture.

non_goal: This registry identifies where admissibility testing applies; it does not assert domain expertise or substitute for domain review.
